creator: grahamperrin@gmail.com
text:
I encountered the symptom this afternoon with an HP ZBook G2, on an HP dock with four side ports and two rear ports. 

Lower rear port: to a hub in a Philips display. From this hub: an HP keyboard and a Kensington Orbit trackball with scroll ring. 

Three side ports: 

- two memory sticks for ZFS L2ARC
- one mobile HDD for a separate ZFS pool. 

After I noticed problems affecting the two sticks in output from 
zpool status -x
I ran these commands in two tabs in Konsole: 

date ; time zpool offline august gpt/cache1-august && zpool clear august gpt/cache1-august && zpool status -x ; date ; tail -f -n 0 /var/log/messages

date ; time zpool offline august gpt/cache2-august && zpool clear august gpt/cache2-august && zpool status -x ; date ; tail -f -n 0 /var/log/messages

After tail appeared in both tabs, I physically unplugged both devices. 

Neither of the tabs showed the log messages that are normal when a device is unplugged. 

Then I realised: 

- the system no longer responded to the external keyboard on USB
- a problem with the pool on the HDD


Mar 16 15:42:59 mowa219-gjp4-zbook-freebsd kernel: Solaris: WARNING: Pool 'Transcend' has encountered an uncorrectable I/O failure and has been suspended.


tl;dr the ZBook keyboard remained functional, shut down failed (no surprise), it was necessary to force off the computer. 

----

Long after the event: 

root@mowa219-gjp4-zbook-freebsd:~ # freebsd-version -kru ; uname -mvKU ; hw-probe -all -upload
15.0-CURRENT
15.0-CURRENT
15.0-CURRENT
FreeBSD 15.0-CURRENT main-n275974-b82d7897595d GENERIC-NODEBUG amd64 1500034 1500034
Probe for hardware ... Ok
Reading logs ... Ok
WARNING: failed to detect EDID
Uploaded to DB, Thank you!

Probe URL: https://bsd-hardware.info/?probe=3ac44e90e5
root@mowa219-gjp4-zbook-freebsd:~ #
